$comment: Catalog of every real schema-change file found in this repo (git ls-files db/ supabase/migrations/), cross-checked against schema.sql (the real schema dump used by testing/contracts/) wherever independently verifiable. Status values: applied-verified (cross-checked against schema.sql and matches), applied-claimed-unverified (file's own header claims applied, not independently checkable from schema alone — e.g. a data migration), pending-do-not-run (file explicitly says not yet approved), status-mismatch (file claims one thing, schema.sql shows another — a real finding, not a template default).
migrationSystemNote: This project does NOT consistently use Supabase CLI-managed, timestamp-ordered migrations. Only 1 of 13 real schema-change files (supabase/migrations/20260630_add_user_email_summaries.sql) lives in the CLI-managed migrations folder with a proper timestamp prefix. The other 12 are ad-hoc SQL files under db/, run manually via the Supabase SQL editor per each file's own header comment, with NO shared ordering, NO migration-tracking table entry, and status tracked only in prose at the top of each file. This is itself the top finding of this phase — see docs/MIGRATION_TESTING.md.

migrations.purpose: Adds authenticated UPDATE policy to japam_history (fixes 403 on History edit sync).
migrations.verificationNote: schema.sql shows "Users can update their own history" policy TO authenticated — present.

migrations.purpose: One-time cleanup of tombstoned-but-not-deleted 'zombie' japam_history rows.
migrations.selfReportedStatus: DO NOT RUN until explicitly approved — review only

migrations.purpose: Migrate legacy numeric Google-sub IDs in groups.created_by/group_members.user_id to canonical UUIDs.
migrations.selfReportedStatus: DO NOT RUN until explicitly approved — review only
migrations.verificationNote: A fresh NUMERIC row was observed in production 2026-07-07 (per prior session's Groups identity-write investigation) — this migration alone would not have prevented that; the write-path fix (repairLegacyStoredUserId wiring) is the code-side complement, already shipped to staging separately.

migrations.purpose: Migrate the 2 cleanly-mappable legacy numeric user_id values in japam_history to their Supabase UUID.
migrations.selfReportedStatus: already applied and validated (dry-run + rollback tested, then real commit, then re-verified via REST API)
migrations.verificationNote: Data migration (row values, not schema) — cannot be independently confirmed from schema.sql alone (would need a row-level query against production, which this task must not run). Self-reported evidence is unusually strong (dry-run + rollback-tested + independently re-verified) relative to other entries here.

migrations.purpose: Adds the deleted_completions tombstone table + policies for offline-safe delete sync.
migrations.selfReportedStatus: run ONCE in SQL editor (implies applied; oldest migration in the set)
migrations.verificationNote: schema.sql shows public.deleted_completions table with RLS enabled and an anon-access policy — present.

migrations.purpose: delete_history_completions RPC — atomic tombstone-write + row-delete in one transaction.
migrations.verificationNote: testing/contracts/structural-contracts.sql confirms delete_history_completions(text[]) exists — verified live via npm run test:contracts, not just this file.

migrations.purpose: Adds groups + group_members tables, base RLS policies, create_group/get_my_groups/find_group_by_invite_code RPCs.
migrations.selfReportedStatus: NOT YET RUN as of writing this file (per its own header comment)
migrations.verificationNote: STATUS MISMATCH, not a template default: schema.sql clearly shows groups/group_members tables and their RPCs live and in use (confirmed via testing/contracts/structural-contracts.sql). The file's own header comment was simply never updated after it was actually run — cosmetic doc drift, not a functional risk, but exactly the class of finding this validation exists to catch.
All three RPCs confirmed present via testing/contracts/structural-contracts.sql.

migrations.purpose: Make get_group_dashboard aware of tombstoned (deleted) japam_history rows so dashboard totals cannot overstate.
migrations.selfReportedStatus: already applied and validated; re-running is safe (CREATE OR REPLACE, idempotent) but not required
migrations.verificationNote: REAL FINDING, unresolved: the live get_group_dashboard function body in schema.sql (verified this session, both by direct inspection and via testing/contracts/structural-contracts.sql's signature check) sums public.japam_history directly with NO reference to deleted_completions/tombstones anywhere in the function body. Either (a) schema.sql predates this migration actually being applied, (b) the migration was applied and later overwritten by some other CREATE OR REPLACE, or (c) it was never actually run despite the header claiming otherwise. Cannot be resolved without a fresh schema dump from production or a direct (read-only) production query, neither of which this task may perform. Flagged as a release risk in testing/risk-map.json (feature=groups) — Group dashboards may currently overstate lifetime/today totals for any user with deleted history rows, matching the previously-catalogued 'Dashboard tombstone awareness' gap (testing/incidents/known-incidents.json: group-dashboard-tombstone-mismatch).

migrations.purpose: Draft server-side schema for Japam Workspaces (currently 100% client-only/AsyncStorage).
migrations.selfReportedStatus: DO NOT RUN until explicitly approved.
migrations.verificationNote: Confirmed consistent: testing/contracts/structural-contracts.sql explicitly asserts NO japams/workspaces table exists server-side, and that assertion currently passes. Matches [[multi-japa-scope]] memory: approved 2026-06-21, parked behind release stabilization, not yet implemented.

migrations.purpose: Adds user_email_summaries table to prevent duplicate summary-email sends.
migrations.selfReportedStatus: run this before enabling email sending (implies applied, given the feature is live)
migrations.verificationNote: schema.sql shows public.user_email_summaries table present with the documented one-row-per-(user,email_type,period_start) comment.

migrations.purpose: Removes anon and cross-user authenticated access to public.japam_history and public.deleted_completions, replacing it with authenticated-only ownership-scoped SELECT/INSERT (japam_history) and SELECT-only (deleted_completions) policies. Guarded, transactional, fails closed if the live baseline doesn't match the confirmed-vulnerable state.
migrations.selfReportedStatus: DO NOT RUN until explicitly approved.
migrations.verificationNote: Phase 3 of the RLS hotfix (Phase 1 client migration to session-JWT auth is already live as of commit 31898616f97ba21a9de9ff742525ad6fdbd6b403 and OTA 019f5dae-2bad-714a-9a69-e91d21c0c9b0). Not yet run against staging or production.
